tfg.gui.spawn_condition.title: Spawn Location
tfg.gui.spawn_condition.tooltip.desert: A land of heat and sand. Surviving here is all about finding the few plants that grow in such a hot and dry climate. If you can make it to LV, you will find the surface oil deposits and flat plains very useful.
tfg.gui.spawn_condition.tooltip.polar: It's extremely cold. The polar climate is desolate, but there are still some flora and fauna that like to live up there. Surviving on an ice sheet will be a challenge in managing heat and will require some creative farming.
tfg.gui.spawn_condition.tooltip.tundra: The tundra is cold, but not too cold. You will be able to find plenty of forests and animals, with some semblance of the seasons. Long-term survival will be a test of how well you prepare and stockpile for winter.
tfg.gui.spawn_condition.tooltip.temperat:
§lThis start is recommended for new players§r

Enjoy the beauty of the seasons. A temperate climate allows many different flora and fauna to live and thrive, as long as they can brace the winter months. This start has a little bit of everything that TerraFirmaCraft has to offer.
tfg.gui.spawn_condition.tooltip.tropical: A 24/7 paradise. Enjoy the lush rainforests and perfect year-round farming conditions. Spawning in the tropics will also make collecting Latex and Kaolin Clay much easier. Survival here should be straightforward, if a bit hot.
tfg.gui.spawn_condition.tooltip.beneath: A true test of your skills. You will spawn and start in the hellish Beneath. While normal progression is possible down here, expect it to be very difficult and require some unorthodox methods to survive and progress.
tfg.gui.spawn_condition.tooltip.default:
§lThis start is recommended for new players§r

The classic you know and love. You can spawn anywhere from subtropical to subpolar, and everything in between. Let the game decide exactly where you spawn, within generally livable conditions.

tfg.tooltip.wireless_card_2: Items can still be transferred through the terminal, but at a very high power cost.
tfg.tooltip.wireless_card_3: Use a §dRailgun§r for your interplanetary logistical needs instead.

tfg.tooltip.component.smr_generator_2:
Dedicated to produce power from sCO2 and the Small Modular Reactor.
 Use Lubricant and Booster to increase its efficiency and manage the cooling loop of the SMR.
tfg.tooltip.machine.fission_reactor_mk1_: §7Customizable Fission Reactor, add better components, process rods and materials into it and watch out for its temperature.§r
tfg.tooltip.machine.smt_2: §7Requires a lubricant to run and can increase power output by using a booster.§r
tfg.tooltip.machine.smt_3: §7Only accept§r SMT Input Hatch §7to input fluids.§r

§7Can only process§r Americium 241 Rod§7 with§r TiCl₄-Doped Supercritical CO₂
tfg.tooltip.machine.heat_battery_2: §7Increase its temperature by inputting hot fluids and use the heat to boil other fluids or run recipes in the OLA.§r
tfg.tooltip.machine.heat_battery_3: §7To transfer Heat to a Ostrum Linear Accelerator, make both§r Heat Port§7 and§r Heat Input Hatch§7 face each other.§r
tfg.tooltip.machine.smr_fluid_import_hat: §7Can only be used in a Small Modular Turbine§r

tfg.tooltip.component.aes_insulation_fra: Can be placed inside a Fission Reactor to increase the maximum heat by §91§r

tfg.tooltip.component.sandy_beryllium_fr: Can be placed inside a Heat Battery to increase its storage ability

quests.ae2.subtitle: A ferramenta mais poderosa para ajudá-lo com GregTech, desbloqueada após sua primeira viagem à lua
quests.ae2.certus_quartz.title: Quartzo Certus
quests.ae2.certus_quartz.subtitle: One small step for you, one giant leap for logistics
quests.ae2.certus_quartz.desc:
With your first step on the Moon comes access to your first &bCertus Quartz&r vein.
Don't worry, they're quite common and should be easy to locate, though having a decent Ore Prospector wouldn't hurt.

Whether you decide to set up a miner or manually dig out a full vein, make sure to gather a hefty amount, as you'll need it to progress through AE2.
quests.ae2.polarizer.title: Polarizer
quests.ae2.polarizer.subtitle: Redstone isn't enough
quests.ae2.polarizer.desc:
One of your main resources for Applied Energistics 2 will be &bCharged Certus Quartz&r.

To obtain it, there’s really only one method: the &6HV&r Polarizer.

You’ll quickly notice how long this recipe takes, so it’s strongly recommended to dedicate a Polarizer exclusively to this task.

It may seem expensive or daunting at first, but don't worry—it’s absolutely worth it.

Also, remember not to charge all your Certus Quartz!
You’ll need some uncharged ones for items like the Annihilation Core, so always keep a small reserve on hand.
quests.ae2.hv_machines.title: The Three Musketeers
quests.ae2.hv_machines.subtitle: Or is it four?
quests.ae2.hv_machines.desc:
The &eSifter&r is used for ore processing to increase your yield of Certus Quartz, Quartzite, Nether Quartz, Ruby, and Armalcolite—all of which are essential for AE2. Alternatively, you can smelt the ores directly in a Furnace if you're in a rush, but you'll lose out on a lot of free resources by doing that.

The second machine, the &eMixer&r, is your only method to create &dFluix Crystals&r (more about that in the next quest). Don’t forget that you can also use the Greate Mixer as a &6HS&r-tier machine.

Thirdly, a &eLaser Engraver&r can combine lesser quality gems together into Exquisite, which will give you better Fluix yields.

Lastly, keep in mind that an &eAutoclave&r can help you generate even more gems from dusts. Once you have the resources, crafting one will make your life significantly easier.
quests.ae2.fluix_crystal.subtitle: Energetic Crystal
quests.ae2.fluix_crystal.desc:
As you can see, there are multiple ways to create Fluix Crystals using a Mixer.

The higher the purity of your input gems, the better the yield — that’s exactly why we recommended crafting a &eSifter&r earlier.

You’ll need a massive amount of Fluix Crystals, especially since the &5liquid version&r is used in most Applied Energistics 2 recipes.

So prepare yourself—you’re going to need a lot!
quests.ae2.extractor.title: Fluid Extractor
quests.ae2.extractor.subtitle: The precious fluix
quests.ae2.extractor.desc:
Most of your Fluix Crystals will be converted into &5Liquid Fluix&r.
However, make sure to keep a few in their crystal form for the occasional recipe that specifically requires it.

Any &eExtractor&r will do the job here — no need for anything fancy.
quests.ae2.fluix_liquid.title: Liquid Fluix
quests.ae2.fluix_liquid.subtitle: The Moon is so cheap
quests.ae2.fluix_liquid.desc:
We told you you'd need a vast quantity of Liquid Fluix… but we didn’t mention there’s a way to drastically increase your yield per Fluix Crystal.

Introducing: &bCryogenized Fluix&r, an ultra-cold fluid only craftable on the Moon using a &bVacuum Freezer&r. Simply combine Liquid Fluix with Helium-3!

This special fluid can be used as a substitute in nearly all recipes that require Liquid Fluix—but only when you're crafting on the Moon.

Moon-based AE2 recipes come with huge benefits: reduced energy costs, less components needed, or sometimes both! Investing in a proper Moon base for AE2 production will pay off massively, especially in early game.

It’s not strictly mandatory — yet. But setting up a second base now will ease your future progression, as planet-based infrastructure becomes essential later on. So… why not get ahead of the curve?
quests.ae2.ae_processor.title: Processors
quests.ae2.ae_processor.subtitle: AE2 Components
quests.ae2.ae_processor.desc:
All three &eProcessors&r will be used extensively across Applied Energistics 2 recipes.

This also gives you a great opportunity to see how effective the &3Moon&r recipes can be for crafting them.

But in the end, the choice is yours — build your infrastructure where it suits you best.
quests.ae2.forming_press.title: Forming Press
quests.ae2.forming_press.subtitle: Small is good too
quests.ae2.forming_press.desc:
To get the printed circuits, you'll need to craft the &6HV&r Forming Press.

The good news? It has multiple input slots, meaning you can insert all your circuit presses at once, so a single Forming Press is enough to handle every printed circuit type.
quests.ae2.ae_press.title: Inscriber Press
quests.ae2.ae_press.subtitle: Treasure hunt
quests.ae2.ae_press.desc:
One of the last things you’ll need before diving fully into Applied Energistics 2 is collecting the 4 &ePresses&r.

To obtain them, you must locate an &eAE2 Meteorite&r on the &3Moon&r — they will look like dark spheres at the bottom of large craters. Luckily, they aren't particularly rare, and a single meteorite will contain all four Presses.

&cBe careful though&r — these meteorites are often guarded, so gear up, bring an ore prospector if you have one, and happy hunting!
quests.ae2.ae_guide.title: Welcome to AE2
quests.ae2.ae_guide.subtitle: Another free book for your collection!
quests.ae2.ae_guide.desc:
Here you are, ready to begin your &bAE2&r infrastructure!

This chapter will mainly guide you through what you can and cannot craft yet.
We’ll also highlight the most important items you should focus on.

However, to fully understand Applied Energistics 2, we strongly recommend using the &bG&r keybind (similar to Create's Ponder system) or open the &bME Guide&r we provide as a reward from this quest. It’s the best in-game tutorial for AE2.
Alternatively, watch some online videos about AE2 on &61.20.1&r for more detailed explanations — AE2 is a very popular mod, so there's a ton of tutorials out there!

&dPro tip&r: Many &bAE2&r recipes have alternative Moon recipes that are much cheaper. Once again, investing in your Moon base will greatly reward you throughout the entire game.
quests.ae2.books_again.title: Need the book again?
quests.ae2.books_again.desc: Here's a repeatable quest if you want a physical copy of the AE2 Guide again.
quests.ae2.hv.title: HV Tier AE2
quests.ae2.hv.desc: This entire branch is fully unlockable in &6HV&r!
quests.ae2.ae_controller.subtitle: The beating heart of your ME network
quests.ae2.ae_controller.desc:
To get started, you’ll need to power your &bME Network&r. These are the two &bAE2&r blocks that can directly accept &bEU&r energy.

We strongly recommend crafting a &bME Controller&r, as &bchannels&r are enabled in TerraFirmaGreg, and the &bME Controller&r is the best way to manage multiple channels efficiently.

This is also a great time to use the in-game guide (press &aU&r on a &bME Controller&r) or watch some videos about &bAE2&r on version 1.20.1 to learn more!
quests.ae2.drive.subtitle: The brains of the network
quests.ae2.drive.desc:
You will need to store your disks, and thanks to the &bMEGA&r addon, you now have an extra option.

You should start with a &bME Drive&r. It can store up to &e10&r disks and consumes &e4 EU/t&r. This will be your main storage solution.

The other option is the &bME Cell Dock&r. It only stores &e1&r disk but consumes just &e1 EU/t&r, and it can be placed directly on a cable like a cover. This makes it quite useful for &aSubnets&r.
quests.ae2.cell_housing.desc:
The &bME Cell Housing&r exists in two versions: one for &aItems&r and one for &9Fluids&r.

You can take their Storage Component out using a &ePacker&r or simply by Sneak + Right-click while holding the storage cell in your hand.
quests.ae2.cables.title: Fluix Cables
quests.ae2.cables.subtitle: The arteries of the network
quests.ae2.cables.desc:
Your basic cables to connect your &bAE2&r infrastructure.

Remember that regular cables carry only &e8 Channels&r, while &6Dense Cables&r can carry up to &a32 Channels&r.

Good news: in &2TFG&r, you can directly craft the &9Smart Cables&r! They are very helpful as they show a &evisual indicator&r for the number of used channels.
quests.ae2.terminals.subtitle: The... fingers? of the network?
quests.ae2.terminals.desc: These terminals are mandatory to access your ME Network and to set up your auto-crafting system.
quests.ae2.molecular_assembler.desc:
The &9Molecular Assembler&r is basically your auto-crafting table.

You can use it for passive crafting, or combine it with a &9Pattern Provider&r.

As always, check the in-game guide using &eG&r to learn more about it.
quests.ae2.crafting_unit.subtitle: The second? brains of the network
quests.ae2.crafting_unit.desc:
The &dCrafting Unit&r works the same way as your &bME Cell Housing&r.

Use a &6Packer&r to make it functional — by itself, it serves no purpose, though you can use it to complete multi-blocks.

It’s your mandatory structure to run your &bAE2&r auto-crafting.

As usual, use the in-game guide from &bAE2&r to learn more about the &dCrafting CPU&r.
quests.ae2.crafting_storage.title: Crafting Storage
quests.ae2.crafting_storage.desc:
These are mandatory for any sort of auto crafting — the bigger the storage, the bigger the recipe chain that can be done with one request. You can also place multiple of them next to each other to add their storage size.

Use the in-game guide for further details — it will explain everything far better than we ever could in a quest.

Take your time to read it, it’s a great resource!
quests.ae2.pattern_provider.title: ME Pattern Provider
quests.ae2.pattern_provider.subtitle: These tell your machines how to craft things
quests.ae2.pattern_provider.desc:
The &9Pattern Provider&r is a modern &bAE2&r block. If you're only familiar with AE2 in previous versions of Minecraft, this is the block that you put your patterns into now instead of an Interface.

It can push items into the connected inventory on a crafting request, and pushing items back into it will send them back to your ME storage.

If you connect these to your GregTech machines, make sure you select the "Allow Input From Output Face" option on them so you can both input and output from the same side!

As always, check the in-game guide using &eG&r to learn more about it.
quests.ae2.pattern.subtitle: Crafting instructions
quests.ae2.pattern.desc:
Each of these tells your ME system how to craft a specific item. You'll need one for every single recipe, so it's worth aiming for the most efficient way to craft a ton of these.

To clear a Pattern, simply Sneak + Right-click them.

Use the in-game guide for full details on how to use them, just remember to set the pattern type to "Processing" for any recipes that'll go in GregTech machines.
quests.ae2.interface.subtitle: I'm giving up on the body part analogy
quests.ae2.interface.desc:
The &dME Interface&r is one of your bread and butter tools to get items or fluids out of, or inside, your network.

If you've only used AE2 in older Minecraft versions, these now only make network contents passively available for other things to pull out of, and don't actively push anything or hold patterns. You'll want the Pattern Provider for that.

The ME Interface can do way too many things to list here, so check the in-game guide using &eG&r to learn more about it.
quests.ae2.interface_automation.title: Interface Automation
quests.ae2.interface_automation.desc:
The best way to passively route items and fluids around your base is through the &bME Interface&r. Since Interfaces don't push out their contents, you'll need to slap a cover on your machine or pipe.

Remember to enable the &eAllow Input&r option on the Output face in your GregTech machine's GUI, in order to push and pull from the same side.

Using filters on your covers can also be a handy way for multiple machines to pull out of the same Interface.

To avoid overfilling a machine's item input slots, you'll need to use a Robot Arm cover on &eKeep Exact&r mode with a filter.

The fluid input doesn’t have this issue, as it will fill only one slot automatically.
quests.ae2.crafting_accelerator.desc:
Simply use the in-game guide — it will explain everything far better than we ever could in a quest.

Take your time to read it, it’s a great resource!
quests.ae2.export_bus.title: Export Bus
quests.ae2.export_bus.desc:
The &dME Export Bus&r is a strong tool to get items or fluids out of your &dME Network&r.
Once you're able to craft them, you should especially look at the &eExtendedAE&r ones, as they can be of great help for some automation.

&cImportant note&r: These have much more of a &4TPS impact&r than something like ME Interfaces, so it's best to use them sparingly.
quests.ae2.import_bus.desc:
The &dME Import Bus&r is one way to get items and fluids into your &dME Network&r. You could also just push items and fluids into an Interface or Pattern Provider for the same result, though, so Import Buses are more niche.

&cImportant note&r: These have much more of a &4TPS impact&r than something like ME Interfaces, so it's best to use them sparingly.
quests.ae2.storage_bus.desc:
The &bME Storage Bus&r is how you tell your ME network about items and fluids stored somewhere outside of ME Drives. They can be very powerful in combination with Super Tanks and Super Chests — just be sure to give the Storage Bus a higher &ePriority&r than your Drive, and partition it to the item or fluid you want to store, so your network tries to put things there first.

Storage Buses are also essential for setting up Subnets — check out the in-game guide for more details on how those work.

Also, &aExtended AE&r comes with a lot of Storage Bus variants that may come in handy later. For example, you could tell your network to "store" all of your &6hot ingots&r in your Vacuum Freezer.
quests.ae2.cell_component_1k.desc:
The &bStorage Component&r will be a core part of your progression through &aAE2&r, and they can be quite expensive, even though you will need them to make your &bStorage Cells&r.

The &e1k ME Storage Component&r is simple enough to not have a specialized moon recipe, but all of the others do, and since the Storage Components get exponentially more expensive... have we convinced you to make a Moon base yet?
quests.ae2.cell_component_4k.desc: Take a look at how the &e4k Storage Component&r has a tremendously cheaper recipe on the &dMoon&r. This will be a consistent trend — the Moon recipes will always use one circuit tier lower than the non-Moon ones.
quests.ae2.cell_component_16k.desc:
The &e16k Storage Component&r is starting to get exponentially more expensive, so now you can really start seeing the savings from having a Moon base.

You do have a Moon base, right?
quests.ae2.p2p.title: P2P Tunneling
quests.ae2.p2p.desc:
Channels are enabled here by default, so you may want to learn about &dP2P&r. They're essentially a way to condense multiple channels down into a single channel, letting you carry hundreds of them across your base through a single cable.

As usual, we advise you to use the in-game guide for more detailed information, and there's also plenty of video tutorials out there that you may find useful.

We also recommend making the &bAdvanced Memory Card&r from &dBetter P2P&r as soon as possible. It will be a big help when you've got many different P2P tunnels to deal with. You can even place a load of &dME P2P Inputs&r on your ME Controller ahead of time, and connect them to an output whenever you need.
quests.ae2.cell_workbench.desc:
The &bCell Workbench&r is used to customize your storage cells.

You can give them &aWhitelist&r/&cBlacklist&r filters or partitions, or add upgrades like the &4Overflow Destruction Card&r — but be cautious with this one!

The &bIO Port&r is used to "defragment" your drives, if you've got the same items scattered across several of them.
quests.ae2.requester.desc.1:
The &aME Requester&r may be one of your &6strongest tools&r if you want to build your automation around &eauto-crafting&r and not passive production.

It can send Crafting Request batches of an item when your network has fewer than a specified amount.

If you are using a lot of &dCrafting Upgrades&r, you'd be better off using this machine as it will have much less of an impact on your &cTPS&r.
quests.ae2.requester.desc.2:
Also note that you can set your Crafting CPUs to be dedicated to Player-started crafting, automatic crafting requests, or neither, which can be useful to stop your Requesters from hogging all your CPUs.

If you like, you can also make a specialized &bTerminal&r to have access to all your Requesters from one place.
quests.ae2.emitters.desc:
&bEmitters&r and the &bToggle Bus&r are your way to manage &cRedstone&r signals in &bAE2&r.

The Level Emitter is especially useful for setting up passive production when combined with the GregTech Machine Controller Cover!

Check the in-game guide for more detailed information.
quests.ae2.batteries.desc: These are the batteries for your ME Network. You may not need them at the beginning, but just keep them in mind — your energy cost could rise quickly, and a blackout in your ME Network can be a really dangerous situation.
quests.ae2.cutting_knife.title: Cutting Knives
quests.ae2.cutting_knife.desc:
These are tools for easily renaming all of your ME components. Why would you want to do this? If you rename your Pattern Providers, their names will show up in the Pattern Terminal, which can be very handy for keeping track of which Pattern Provider is connected to which machine.

For example &oSmall Gear Extruder&r as a name for the Pattern Provider would let you easily know that this Extruder contains a Small Gear mold, so you should only put Small Gear patterns in it.
quests.ae2.pattern_box.desc: The Pattern Box can carry around all your Blank and Encoded Patterns in one inventory slot, and they will be visible in any AE2 device UI as long as the box is somewhere in your inventory.
quests.ae2.wireless_access.title: ME Wireless Network
quests.ae2.wireless_access.subtitle: Get Free WiFi Anywhere You Go
quests.ae2.wireless_access.desc:
Once you've made it to &5EV&r, you'll gain access to the ME Wireless Access Point, which basically acts like a WiFi Router for your ME network. Connect it to a central point in your base, shove as many Wireless Booster cards as you want to increase its range (and power draw), and then link a Wireless Terminal to it.

Once you have Epoxy and Rhodium, you'll be able to combine all of your Wireless Terminals together via a Universal Terminal!
quests.ae2.interplanetary_wireless.desc:
You can upgrade your Wireless Terminal with this special Card, allowing access to your network from any distance, even on different worlds! Sounds too good to be true? There's a major catch — transferring items uses a huge amount of energy.

You can sort of get around this with some Energy Cards and a battery in your inventory set to Discharge Mode, but it should work well enough for those times when you accidentally left something at your base on Earth and don't want to fly all the way back.

For proper automated transfer, you'll need a Railgun.
quests.ae2.universal_circuits.title: Universal Circuits
quests.ae2.universal_circuits.subtitle: Oh so THAT's what these are for!
quests.ae2.universal_circuits.desc:
Ever wondered what the point of the Universal Circuit items was? Besides nice quest icons, they're primarily used for AE2!

AE2 doesn't handle item tags in patterns well, so the universal circuits are designed to be used in any patterns that require circuits. This way, whenever you unlock a new kind of circuit, you only need to add one pattern to convert the new circuit into the universal one, instead of updating &oall&r of your old patterns!
quests.ae2.stock_bridge.title: AE Stock Bridge
quests.ae2.stock_bridge.desc:
If you invested heavily into Create's logistics network, you might despair at the thought of having to tear it all down to replace it with AE2. The &bStock Bridge&r acts as a bridge between your ME network and your Create one. Check its Ponder to learn how to set it up.

We still recommend moving over any old setups to AE2 eventually, mostly because it's much lighter on your game's performance.
quests.ae2.iv.title: IV Tier AE2
quests.ae2.iv.desc: This entire branch becomes fully unlockable once you reach &1IV&r.
quests.ae2.accumulation_press.desc:
To get this &eNew Inscriber Press&r, you will need to invest a bit.

First, you must upgrade your Implosion Compressor with &5two EV Energy Hatches&r.

Then, get yourself a new IV Laser Engraver if you don't already have one.

And finally, prepare a lot of Industrial TNT — it’s the &conly one that works&r for this process.

Plan carefully, as this step is quite demanding!
quests.ae2.accumulation_processor.desc:
The &eAccumulation Processor&r will require a large amount of Silicon for each craft.

We hope you already have a dedicated EBF producing Silicon, or at least a strong infrastructure to manufacture it quickly.

Prepare accordingly, as you’ll need plenty of it!
quests.ae2.mega_crafting.desc:
These are a neat upgrade for your &bCrafting CPU&r.

They will help to speed up your auto-crafting and allow you to handle larger jobs with ease.
quests.ae2.mega_battery.desc: A &ebigger battery&r for a bigger network. Nothing else to it!
quests.ae2.extended_pattern_access.desc:
The &eExtended Pattern Terminal&r is a wonderful upgrade to manage all your patterns in an easier way.

You get &6more search options&r for your patterns, and with how many you should be getting now, it will be a huge QoL improvement.

Make the "upgrade" version to simply sneak-right-click your existing pattern providers to upgrade them without having to take all the patterns out.
quests.ae2.extended_pattern_access.task: Either the part or the upgrade
quests.ae2.extended_interface.desc:
Here they are, your bigger &bInterface&r with an upgrade so you don't even need to disconnect them to upgrade.

You will be quite happy with them!
quests.ae2.extended_interface.task: Either the part or the upgrade
quests.ae2.extended_patprov.desc:
Here they are, your bigger &bPattern Providers&r with an upgrade so you don't even need to disconnect them to upgrade.

You will be quite happy with them!
quests.ae2.extended_patprov.task: Either the part or the upgrade
quests.ae2.extended_drive.desc:
The &dME Extended Drive&r will &6double&r the amount of &eStorage Cells&r you can have within it.

You can also craft the &eupgrade&r to simply &aRight Click&r your existing ME Drives without having to take your discs out, and it won't cost you more.
quests.ae2.extended_drive.task: Either the part or the upgrade
quests.ae2.extended_import_export.desc: These are eight times faster than their regular counterparts. Enjoy!
quests.ae2.extended_import_export.task: Either the part or the upgrade
quests.ae2.extended_io_port.desc: This upgrade will let you &atransfer faster&r while also offering &6more upgrade slots&r if needed.
The upgrade of the &bMolecular Assembler&r.

It runs &68 crafting jobs&r at the same time and goes &62 times faster&r.

It will be a &agreat upgrade&r before you get the &dMatrix Multiblock&r a bit later on.
quests.ae2.wireless.desc:
The &aME Wireless Connector&r is your go-to if you don't want to put down massive lines of &bME Cables&r. They effectively work like short-range Quantum Tunnels, but have a limited range and don't work across dimensions. That would be too easy, wouldn't it?

Don't forget to check the in-game guide with &eG&r to learn about how they work and their limitations.
quests.ae2.mega_cell_housing.desc: These &bCell Housings&r work the same way as the basic ones, but you will need these to make the bigger disks from &dMEGA&r.
quests.ae2.cell_component_64k.desc: Now that you have &1IV&r Circuits, you can make this enormous &5Storage Component&r. Aren't you glad you made a Moon base?
quests.ae2.cell_component_256k.desc:
The &c256k ME Storage Component&r is the last one you can make with a regular Assembler, and it's so big that you may never need any of the bigger sizes.

Unless you're up for the challenge, of course.
quests.ae2.luv.title: LuV Tier AE2
quests.ae2.luv.desc: This branch becomes unlockable once you reach &dLuV&r.
quests.ae2.cell_component_1m.desc:
The &3MEGA Storage Components&r will need an &bAssembly Line&r. Note that the non-Moon recipe must be crafted within a Cleanroom, or you can use a Cleaning Maintenance Hatch if you have access to one.

No need for that on the Moon though.
quests.ae2.cell_component_16m.desc:
This one can only be made on the &cMoon&r and nowhere else. It will also require a Cleanroom environment, so if you don’t want to bother with a Cleanroom on the &cMoon&r, you should make the &7Cleaning Maintenance Hatch&r.

It will also need some research. Refer to the &cZPM&r Chapter if you don’t know what that means.
quests.ae2.cell_component_64m.desc: The &c64M MEGA Storage Component&r is the biggest one you can make in &cTerraFirmaGreg&r. Honestly, we believe you'll never need it, but it’s there for completionists.
quests.ae2.oversize_interface.desc: The &dME Oversize Interface&r is a &dME Extended Interface&r but each slot can hold 16x more items or fluids. If you have processes that need huge quantities, this could be your go-to.
quests.ae2.assembler_matrix.desc:
The &6Assembler Matrix&r is basically a customizable multiblock Molecular Assembler. The requirements for this quest should let you build one that we deem quite strong enough. You could add more layers of &bPattern Core&r or &aCraft Core&r if you want to.

As usual, check the in-game guide for detailed informations.
quests.combat_tips.stone_main.title: Stone Age
quests.combat_tips.hammer.title: Hammers
quests.combat_tips.hammer.subtitle: It's hammer time!
quests.combat_tips.hammer.description:
While combat may not be the first thing you think to do with your hammer, it can be surprisingly effective. Hammers deal &2Crushing&r damage which can help you against Skeletons. 

For more information on &cDamage Types&r consult the &3Field Guide&r.
quests.combat_tips.javelin.title: Javelins
quests.combat_tips.javelin.subtitle: Caveman's bow
quests.combat_tips.javelin.description:
If you want to keep your foes away from you, javelins are the weapon for you. They are the earliest ranged weapons you will have available to you. They deal &9Piercing&r damage, which can help you against Zombies. 

For more information on &cDamage Types&r consult the &3Field Guide&r. 

Once you acquire leather you will be able to craft a &5Quiver&r which allows you to have easy access to up to 8 javelins!
quests.combat_tips.axe.title: Axes
quests.combat_tips.axe.subtitle: Good for more than wood
quests.combat_tips.axe.description:
Until you can upgrade, an axe will be your most reliable weapon. Along with high damage, they deal &6Slashing&r damage, which will help you against Creepers. 

For more information on &cDamage Types&r consult the &3Field Guide&r.
quests.combat_tips.knife.title: Knives
quests.combat_tips.knife.subtitle: Stabby stabby
quests.combat_tips.knife.description:
Knives are most useful for crafting, but they can be a weapon in a pinch. They deal &9Piercing&r damage, which can help you against Zombies. 

For more information on &cDamage Types&r consult the &3Field Guide&r.
quests.combat_tips.club.title: Flint Club
quests.combat_tips.club.subtitle: Gronk Bonk
quests.combat_tips.club.description:
The flint clubs are rather unconventional weapons. Instead of dealing damage, they will break your target's bones, stopping them in their tracks.

Both the standard and fortified flint clubs can be crafted with primitive materials. The fortified flint club boasts a faster attack speed and more durability.
quests.combat_tips.mold_main.title: Primitive Metallurgy
quests.combat_tips.wood_shield.title: Wooden Shield
quests.combat_tips.wood_shield.subtitle: Skeletons hurt, like a lot
quests.combat_tips.wood_shield.descripti: A shield will protect you from most damage sources you point it at. Be warned, it will &4not&r protect you from explosions.
quests.combat_tips.leather.title: Leather Armor
quests.combat_tips.leather.subtitle: Clothes at last
quests.combat_tips.leather.description:
Once you have leather, you will be able to get your first true armor. It isn't great, but it will provide that extra protection you may need. 

They also give you a slight resistance to &6Slashing&r damage.
quests.combat_tips.mold_weapons.title: Molded Weapons
quests.combat_tips.mold_weapons.subtitle: The power of metal
quests.combat_tips.mold_weapons.descript:
Molds allow you to make copper and bronze versions of all weapons, including three new types: Swords, Scythes, and Maces. 

Swords: These well rounded weapons deal &6Slashing&r damage. 

Scythes: They do more damage than swords, but are slower. They deal &6Slashing&r damage. 

Maces: They are the best weapons that deal &2Crushing&r damage.
quests.combat_tips.bow.title: Bow and Arrow
quests.combat_tips.bow.subtitle: Then I took an arrow to the knee...
quests.combat_tips.bow.description:
While javelins are cool, it's hard to beat a bow and arrow. Arrows can be expensive, so setting up a bird farm isn't a bad idea. Arrows deal &9Piercing&r damage. 

Once you have leather you will be able to craft a &5Quiver&r which allows you to have easy access to up to 8 stacks of arrows!
quests.combat_tips.bronze_main.title: Bronze Age
quests.combat_tips.forged_weapons.title: Forged Weapons
quests.combat_tips.forged_weapons.descri:
From wrought iron and above you will need the appropriate anvil to forge them. Additionally, the anvil unlocks butchery knives. 

Butchery Knives: Faster than swords, but do less damage. They allow you to get &cdouble&r meat from mobs. This doesn't affect any other drops.
quests.combat_tips.shield.title: Metal Shield
quests.combat_tips.shield.subtitle: Premium protection
quests.combat_tips.shield.description:
Now these are some real shields. Using one of these will protect you from any damage, including explosions. 

They also come in a bunch of fun shapes!
quests.combat_tips.armor.title: Metal Armor
quests.combat_tips.armor.subtitle: Only the shiniest suit of armor
quests.combat_tips.armor.description:
Metal armor requires a two-step forging process, but don't be scared off by its difficulty. Metal armor will boost your survival chances going into the &dBeneath&r or if you fight off a group of &3Illagers&r.

All metal armor will give you bonus protection to &6Slashing&r, &9Piercing&r, and &2Crushing&r damage
quests.combat_tips.iron_main.title: Iron Age
quests.combat_tips.crossbow.title: Crossbow
quests.combat_tips.crossbow.subtitle: It's like a manual gun
quests.combat_tips.crossbow.description: Quite simply, it functions the same as the vanilla crossbow. Similar to the bow you can use a &5Quiver&r to hold up to 8 stacks of arrows.
quests.combat_tips.flintlock_mechanism.d:
This is your first &2Gun Mechanism&r, which are the main crafting components of most guns. The &6Flintlock Mechanism&r allows you to make your first two guns! The first of which is the &9Flintlock Pistol&r.

Once you acquire steel you will be able to make a much cheaper &6Flintlock Mechanism&r.
quests.combat_tips.flintlock_gun.title: Flintlock Pistol
quests.combat_tips.flintlock_gun.descrip:
Well this is the moment you have been waiting for, it's a gun. Now it's not that good of a gun, but technology has to start somewhere. Guns have many advantages compared to other ranged weapons, useful to you now is that &3Glayzes&r and &2Klayzes&r cannot block bullets.

For more information on how TACZ guns work view the gun at the top of this quest chapter.
quests.tasktype.checkmark: Clique aqui para completar essa missão/tarefa
quests.computer.subtitle: ComputerCraft ou CC: Tweaked aqui, se você sabe programar em Lua, esse é um dos mods mais fortes para gerenciar sua base
quests.create_logistics.subtitle: Gestão de armazém para leigos
quests.create_logistics.introduction.des:
Utilizando algumas máquinas do &3Create&r, você poderá melhorar significativamente suas capacidades de transporte e armazenamento de itens. Mais tarde, ao avançar para a &2Era Voltaica&r, você poderá até configurar processos para encomendar itens específicos automaticamente ou distribuí-los pela sua base conforme necessário!

Como esta série de missões lida principalmente com máquinas do Create, isso significa que o &5Pondering&r sobre as máquinas será sua melhor fonte de informações além dessas missões, então não deixe de conferir as entradas.
quests.create_logistics.packager.title: Embalador
quests.create_logistics.packager.desc.1:
Ao receber um sinal de &credstone&r, o &3Embalador&r embrulhará os itens do bloco ao qual está conectado (você pode trocar o lado da conexão com uma &5chave inglesa&r) e produzirá um pequeno pacote de papelão. Cada pacote de papelão tem 9 espaços de inventário, sendo ótimo para transportar vários itens de uma só vez. Para fabricar o Embalador, você precisa de bastante &dFerro Forjado&r e um &dTubo de Elétrons&r, sem mencionar um monte de &dPapelão&r (&dPapel&r e &dCola&r).

Observe que, uma vez fabricado, o embalador funciona sem nenhum papelão adicional, então não se preocupe com a manutenção!
quests.create_logistics.packager.desc.2:
Adicionar uma &3Placa&r ao Embalador fará com que ele rotule automaticamente o conteúdo da placa em todos os pacotes como um endereço de destino. Isso será mais útil quando você começar a trabalhar com várias maneiras de rotear pacotes. 

Os Embaladores também podem desembrulhar quaisquer pacotes que sejam colocados nele, descarregando seu conteúdo no inventário anexo.

&c&lObserve que Placas Emolduradas não funcionam para esse propósito&r!
quests.create_logistics.frogport.title: Pacote Frogports
quests.create_logistics.frogport.desc.1: Os &3Pacotes de Frogports&r são uma das três maneiras de mover pacotes e levá-los até onde você deseja. Eles podem pendurar pacotes em linhas de &3Transportadores de Correntes&r, que podem ser transportados ao longo das conexões de corrente. Para usá-los, você precisa primeiro de uma rede de &3Transportadores de Correntes&r conectadas entre si por correntes e, em seguida, energizados: qualquer corrente com a tag &d#forge:chains&r serve. Clique com o botão direito do mouse em uma conexão de corrente antes de colocar um Frogport para que ele interaja com esse segmento da corrente. Os Frogports colocados diretamente sobre um Embalador penduram automaticamente os pacotes criados no Embalador.
quests.create_logistics.frogport.desc.2:
Os Frogports também podem receber pacotes, além de enviá-los. Se você clicar com o botão direito do mouse em um Frogport, verá que ele possui um nome editável. Este nome funciona como um &aendereço&r - se algum pacote rotulado com um endereço de destino corresponder ao seu nome, o Frogport os &acoletará automaticamente&r, removendo-os da rede em cadeia! Este pacote coletado será colocado no inventário do bloco em que o Frogport estiver localizado.

Enquanto a rede em cadeia estiver conectada às entradas e saídas do Frogports &osomehow&r, o pacote eventualmente encontrará seu caminho, embora você possa otimizar os caminhos para reduzir o tempo de viagem do pacote por conta própria.
quests.create_logistics.package_filter.s: A solução geral para roteamento de pacotes
quests.create_logistics.package_filter.d: Os pacotes podem ser roteados usando o &3Filtro de Pacotes&r especializado, que pode ser usado como qualquer filtro em qualquer máquina &3Create&r que suporte slots de filtro. Basta clicar com o botão direito do mouse enquanto segura o filtro e digitar o endereço a ser filtrado, e todos os pacotes com esse endereço de destino especificado serão liberados. Isso é útil especialmente para &3Túneis de Latão&r para que sua rede de correias roteie seus pacotes com base no destino.
quests.create_logistics.postbox.title: Caixas de Correio
quests.create_logistics.postbox.subtitle: Você um mail
quests.create_logistics.postbox.desc.1:
&3Caixas de Correio&r são usadas para rotear pacotes usando &aRedes de Trem&r. Primeiro, clique com o botão direito do mouse em uma &3Estação de Trem&r para vincular a Caixa de Correio a essa estação antes de posicioná-la. A partir daí, qualquer trem que pare lá poderá interagir com a Caixa de Correio (desde que esteja a até 4 quarteirões da estação). 

Assim como os &3Portos de Pacotes&r, eles podem ser nomeados. Os trens que passarem coletarão quaisquer pacotes cujo destino não corresponda ao nome da Caixa de Correio. Além disso, se o trem tiver pacotes em seus inventários que &odo&r correspondam à Caixa de Correio, eles serão passados para o inventário da Caixa de Correio.
quests.create_logistics.postbox.desc.2: Uma última coisa legal sobre &3Postboxes&r é que ao extrair automaticamente delas (por meio de calhas, funis, etc.), apenas os pacotes com um destino que &acorda com o nome da Postbox&r serão retirados e quaisquer pacotes com um destino diferente permanecerão dentro para serem carregados nos trens mais tarde.
quests.create_logistics.nbt_handling.sub: Outras informações sobre pacotes e endereços
quests.create_logistics.nbt_handling.des:
Aqui estão algumas outras coisas importantes que podem ser úteis ao tentar rotear pacotes:

&3-&r Os pacotes são criados aleatoriamente a partir de uma seleção de quatro itens possíveis, então recursos como os &3Filtros de Itens&r da GregTech serão &cmuito tediosos de configurar&r! Recomendamos usar o &3Filtro de Pacotes&r sempre que possível, mas...

&3-&r Se você realmente quiser tentar usar outros métodos para mover pacotes, o endereço de destino é armazenado como dados NBT na tag &eAddress&r. Por exemplo, um pacote com o endereço de destino "Casa" terá em seus dados NBT: &e{Address:"Casa"}&r.

&3-&r A filtragem de endereços de pacotes suporta uma forma simplificada de &eRegEx&r. Isso significa que você pode:

&e-&r Corresponder a qualquer string de comprimento com *

&e-&r Corresponder a qualquer caractere individual com ?

&e-&r Corresponder a um único caractere de um grupo de caracteres cercado por [] (por exemplo, A[cr]t corresponderá a 'Act' e 'Art', mas não a 'Ant')

&e-&r Corresponder a um único caractere que não pertença a um grupo de caracteres cercado por [! ] (por exemplo, A[!cr]t corresponderá a 'Aft' e 'Apt', mas não a 'Act' ou 'Art')

&e-&r Corresponde a uma string de uma lista de strings fornecida dentro de {}(por exemplo, TerraFirma{Craft,Greg} corresponderá a TerraFirma&eCraft&r e TerraFirma&eGreg&r)

&e-&r Todos os símbolos anteriores podem ser escapados; Por exemplo, A\* será lido como A* e corresponderá somente a 'A*'.
quests.create_logistics.bottler.title: Engarrafador
quests.create_logistics.bottler.subtitle: Um embalador, só que para fluidos
quests.create_logistics.bottler.desc: O Bottler é o equivalente fluido do embalador. Coloque-o próximo a qualquer tipo de recipiente de fluido (tanque de fluido, tambores, máquinas e assim por diante) e coloque um Frogport em cima do Bottler, e o resto funcionará como esperado! O Bottler no destino também pode empurrar diretamente para recipientes de fluido, sem a necessidade de bomba!
quests.create_logistics.rose_quartz.desc:
Mover pacotes é bom e tudo mais, mas certamente não pode ser só isso, não é? Bem, você está absolutamente certo. Há muito mais que pode ser feito com pacotes, incluindo &aler o conteúdo do inventário&r e &afazer com que as coisas sejam entregues remotamente&r, e mais tarde até mesmo &aenviar ingredientes automaticamente para serem processados&r com base no limite de um item!

Mas antes de nos aprofundarmos em &2Alta Logística&r, alguns preparativos são necessários.

Os &dTransmissores&r que seriam necessários para criar tais equipamentos só podem ser produzidos realisticamente por meio de luz de alta energia refratada por um mineral único: &dQuartzo Rosa&r. Em outras palavras, precisamos de um &7Gravador a Laser Básico&r e de alguma forma de obter uma &dLente de Quartzo Rosa&r, então é melhor você começar a fazer isso, certo?
quests.create_logistics.cold_rose_quartz:
A única fonte natural de &dQuartzo Rosa&r com qualidade alta o suficiente para criar facilmente a &dLente&r está localizada muito, muito ao norte, em temperaturas congelantes (abaixo de &b-10°C&r). Já teve os flashbacks da argila de caulim? 

O Quartzo Rosa só se forma no núcleo de geleiras antigas, o que significa que você terá que procurar onde elas eventualmente se depositam - praias, lagos, planícies e até mesmo debaixo d'água, nos oceanos. Embora não seja comum, o núcleo da geleira é facilmente identificado por suas camadas externas de &bGelo Azul&r. O &dQuartzo Rosa&r será encontrado em seu interior - primeiro como minério, depois como blocos de minério bruto comprimido bem no centro.

O que precisamos é de &dQuartzo Rosa Exótico&r, que você pode obter &3triturando, lavando e peneirando&r o minério bruto que você minerou; A gema Exótica tem 3% de chance de ser peneirada, mas você só precisa de uma gema para prosseguir. 

&c&lNão funda o minério, pois isso só lhe dará quartzo rosa de qualidade normal&r!

Os mesmos preparativos que você pode ter feito para a argila de caulim também se aplicam aqui: prepare bastante comida, uma fonte de água potável e algum meio de transporte (barcos são bons, mas aeronaves devem estar bem ao seu alcance neste momento). Você também pode considerar levar alguns equipamentos de mergulho de cobre. O capacete permitirá que você respire debaixo d'água quando combinado com um tanque de cobre (você precisa enchê-lo com ar pressurizado, colocando-o no chão e energizando-o com a força rotacional). As botas permitirão que você atravesse debaixo d'água com mais facilidade. Os núcleos de geleiras são frequentemente encontrados no oceano e podem ser bastante trabalhosos para minerar sem esses preparativos (embora também possam ser encontrados em terra).
quests.create_logistics.rose_quartz_lens: Independentemente de como você obteve os materiais necessários, você precisará de um &3Torno&r para criar a lente. Um &7Torno &3Básico será suficiente para criar a lente a partir de uma gema de &dQuartzo Rosa Requintado&r, mas se você quiser fazer a lente a partir de uma simples placa, precisará de um equipamento melhor, como um &bTorno &3Avançado&r. Depois de fabricada, a lente pode ser usada em uma &7Gravadora a Laser Básica&r &dPlaca de Liga Dupla Vermelha&r para criar a unidade &dTransmissora&r necessária, e não será consumida no processo.
quests.create_logistics.stock_link.title: Link de Ações
quests.create_logistics.stock_link.subti: Temos barramentos de armazenamento em casa
quests.create_logistics.stock_link.desc:
A primeira máquina de &2Logística Avançada&r que você pode criar é um &3Link de Estoque&r, que pode ser usado para criar uma &dRede Logística&r. Quando colocado em qualquer lado de um &3Embalador&r, o Link de Estoque &tornará o inventário ao qual o Embalador está conectado visível para a rede&r! Por si só, o Link de Estoque não é muito útil, mas é um componente essencial de todas as outras máquinas que virão...

Lembre-se de clicar com o botão direito em um Link de Estoque existente antes de instalar um para sintonizar os dois e compartilhar a mesma rede. Para cancelar isso, você pode simplesmente craftar o Link de Estoque sozinho para limpar sua sintonização.
quests.create_logistics.stock_ticker.sub: Garçom! Garçom! Mais Terra Siltosa Argilosa Enraizada, por favor!
quests.create_logistics.stock_ticker.des: Se você já usou &3Links de Estoque&r para fornecer vários itens a uma &dRede Logística&r, o próximo passo é criar algo que possa chamar esses itens remotamente... E é aí que entra o &3Marcador de Estoque&r. Assim como o Link de Estoque, primeiro você precisa clicar com o botão direito em um link existente para sintonizar o Marcador a essa rede. Em seguida, você precisa colocar um &3Queimador Blaze&r ou qualquer mob em um &3Assento&r ao lado do Marcador de Estoque para que eles funcionem como &3Guardião de Estoque&r.

Agora que a pobre criatura está sob contrato vitalício, você pode clicar nela para ver todos os itens na &dRede Logística&r. Você pode até procurar itens na rede e criar um pedido para ser enviado para endereços específicos! Não há limite de alcance para isso, embora você precise criar sua própria maneira de rotear os pacotes que serão pedidos pelos Links de Estoque, além de ter o link em questão carregado em blocos para que isso funcione.
Além disso, você pode configurar o Marcador para que apenas itens específicos sejam exibidos ou configurar categorias de itens.
quests.create_logistics.redstone_request:
O &3Solicitador de Redstone&r pode ser visto como um &3Marcador de Estoque&r mais especializado. Você não pode visualizar itens na &dRede Logística&r a partir dele, mas pode configurar uma ordem para ser solicitada automaticamente sempre que a máquina receber um sinal de redstone. Isso pode ser muito útil para configurar o fornecimento automatizado de itens específicos - considere usar um &3Interruptor de Limiar&r ou um &3Observador Inteligente&r junto com ele, por exemplo.

Lembre-se de que, como qualquer outra máquina de &2Logística Avançada&r, você precisa clicar com o botão direito em um link existente antes de colocar o Solicitante para baixo para sintonizá-lo com essa rede primeiro!
quests.create_logistics.tablecloths.subt: Eles asfaltaram o paraíso e construíram um shopping center
quests.create_logistics.tablecloths.desc:
&3Toalhas de Mesa&r é o sistema do Create para lojas de jogadores! Cada Toalha de Mesa pode vender um item. Você precisará de uma pequena configuração com um Marcador de Estoque, um Embalador e um Guardião de Estoque (um Queimador de Blaze ou um monstro em um assento) para começar.

Clique com o botão direito do mouse no Guardião de Estoque com sua Toalha de Mesa e selecione o item a ser vendido. Em seguida, coloque a Toalha de Mesa sobre um bloco ou andaime. Depois de colocá-la, clique com o botão direito do mouse no espaço na frente da Toalha de Mesa com um item que você deseja como pagamento. Em seguida, clique com o botão direito e segure para alterar o valor desse item.

Para comprar itens da loja de outro jogador, clique com o botão direito nas Toalhas de Mesa com a mão vazia para adicioná-las a uma Lista de Compras. Quando estiverem prontas, clique com o botão direito no Guardião de Estoque com a Lista de Compras para comprar os itens! Uma Lista de Compras pode ser limpa com um clique com o botão direito segurando agachar. O pagamento é então armazenado no Marcador de Estoque, e o lojista pode retirá-lo mais tarde ou enviá-lo de volta para sua base!
quests.create_logistics.factory_gauge.de:
O &3Medidor de Fábrica&r é a máquina definitiva de &2Logística Avançada&r, exigindo que você tenha a tecnologia para fabricar &dAlumínio&r e &dDiodos&r antes de poder fabricá-lo. Mas ele é extremamente poderoso.

A primeira coisa que um Medidor de Fábrica pode fazer é ser colocado em um &3Embalador&r para monitorar a quantidade de um item específico no inventário conectado. Quando configurado dessa forma, a borda externa do medidor será &9Azul&r e diz-se que está no &9Modo Reabastecedor&r.

A outra maneira de usar um Medidor de Fábrica é fazer com que ele peça ingredientes automaticamente quando a quantidade de produtos-alvo em uma &dRede Logística&r cair abaixo de um limite. Este é o modo de operação quando o medidor é colocado em qualquer bloco que não seja um Embalador; a borda externa será &eAmarelo&r e diz-se que está no &9Modo Receita&r.

Em qualquer um dos modos, você pode definir um valor específico que o Indicador de Fábrica deve tentar manter. Em seguida, você precisa definir um endereço de destino na interface gráfica do usuário (GUI), para onde o pedido automático será direcionado para um endereço de destino de sua escolha.

No &9Modo Reestoque&r, o endereço especificado deve, idealmente, apontar para o embalador na qual o indicador foi colocado, para que os pacotes recebidos sejam desembrulhados automaticamente no estoque de destino.

Para o &9Modo Receita&r, o endereço especificado deve apontar para onde você fabrica o produto.

Além disso, você precisa configurar um medidor adicional para cada tipo de ingrediente usado e conectá-los ao primeiro medidor com &a'Adicionar Nova Conexão'&r na GUI.
Se o produto que você está tentando criar no &9Modo de Receita&r for uma receita de criação normal, você precisará &a'Usar Criação Mecânica'&r e garantir que o pacote esteja desembrulhado em uma grade 3x3 de &3Artesões Mecânicos&r.

&cÉ importante que o pacote não seja desembrulhado durante o trajeto&r, pois o próprio pacote contém instruções de receitas de criação - se os ingredientes precisarem vir de fontes diferentes, considere usar um &3Re-embalador&r para atuar como um buffer para os pacotes.

&3Observação:&r As solicitações do Medidor de Fábrica são tecnicamente 'Promessas', que são garantias da rede de que os itens em questão &oserão&r entregues em algum momento no futuro. Isso é feito para evitar que os itens sejam solicitados em excesso. Por padrão, essas promessas nunca expiram; isso significa que a promessa permanecerá em vigor para sempre se os itens provavelmente não serão entregues no futuro. Você pode alterar esse comportamento ajustando a seção inferior da interface gráfica do usuário (GUI) do medidor, o que limitará a duração das promessas.
quests.development.subtitle: Uma forma simples de entender sua progressão no GregTech
quests.development.explanation.title: Vamos dar algumas explicações
quests.development.explanation.subtitle: É na verdade bem fácil
quests.development.explanation.desc.1:
A &lProgressão de Circuito&r do GregTech pode parecer intimidadora inicialmente, mas não é tão ruim assim.

Primeiro, você precisa entender que circuitos do mesmo nível são intercambeáveis. Por exemplo, para craftar um &6Misturador HV&r, você pode usar qualquer circuito de nível &6HV&r.
quests.development.explanation.desc.2:
Agora, vamos falar sobre &lprogressão&r. Como você pode notar, cada circuito compartilha um código de cor em seu ícone e um nome. Por exemplo, todos os &aProcessadores Quânticos&r são &averdes&r e criados dentro de Montadores de Circuitos de nível &1IV&r. Para criar um, você precisa primeiro do circuito anterior — é por isso que, para criar seu primeiro &dCircuito LuV&r, o Mainframe Nanoprocessador, você deve começar criando um Nanoprocessador e progredir em cada &7etapa&r. Nesse caso, seriam necessários três passos.

É por isso que os &6circuitos&r mais à direita em cada linha são sempre mais baratos para &acriar&r: eles exigem menos &7etapas&r.
quests.development.explanation.desc.3: Além disso, adicionamos &lCircuitos Universais&r. Eles podem ser &a criados&r a partir de qualquer &6circuito&r — seja &1IV&r, &dLuV&r, &3UV&r ou qualquer outro nível — e são extremamente úteis para &9automação&r com o AE2. Tente codificar todos os seus &fpadrões&r com &lCircuitos Universais&r, assim, sempre que você atualizar para um circuito melhor, não precisará refazer todos os seus &fpadrões&r — apenas os usados para criar o Circuito Universal.
quests.development.explanation.task: Ok, li as três páginas inteiras
quests.development.ulv.title: Circuitos &8ULV&r
quests.development.ulv.desc: Nesta linha você pode ver os equivalentes do Vapor ou &8ULV&r.
quests.development.lv.title: Circuitos &7LV&r
quests.development.lv.desc:
Os circuitos dessa linha são &7LV&r e são intercambiáveis em receitas.

&cCircuitos Eletrônicos&r são os &piores&r em termos de custo e esforço necessários para produzir, mas você tem que começar de algum lugar.

&eProcessadores Integrados Básicos&r atuam como uma camada intermediária entre &cCircuitos Eletrônicos&r e &2Processadores Microchip.&r

Finalmente, &2Processadores Microchip&r serão a melhor opção para o resto do jogo.
quests.development.mv.title: Circuitos &bMV&r
quests.development.mv.desc:
Os circuitos ao longo desta linha são &bMV&r e são intercambiáveis em receitas.

O padrão permanece o mesmo: o circuito mais à direita vem em &dquantidades maiores&r e é, no geral, &amais fácil&r de produzir.
quests.development.hv.title: Circuitos &6HV&r
quests.development.hv.desc:
Os circuitos dessa linha são &6HV&r e são intercambiáveis em receitas.

O padrão permanece o mesmo: o circuito mais à direita vem em &dquantidades maiores&r e é, no geral, &amais fácil&r de produzir.
quests.development.ev.title: Circuitos &5EV&r
quests.development.ev.desc:
Os circuitos dessa linha são &5EV&r e são intercambiáveis em receitas.

O padrão permanece o mesmo: o circuito mais à direita vem em &dquantidades maiores&r e é, no geral, &amais fácil&r de produzir.
quests.development.iv.title: Circuitos &1IV&r
quests.development.iv.desc:
Os circuitos dessa linha são &1IV&r e são intercambiáveis em receitas.

O padrão permanece o mesmo: o circuito mais à direita vem em &dquantidades maiores&r e é, no geral, &amais fácil&r de produzir.
quests.development.luv.title: Circuitos &dLuV&r
quests.development.luv.desc:
Circuitos nessa linha são &dLuV&r e são intercambiáveis em receitas.

O padrão permanece o mesmo: o circuito mais à direita vem em &dmaiores quantidades&r e é, no geral, &amais fácil&r de produzir.

Certifique-se de configurar a &3produção de placas de wetware&r o mais rápido possível! Elas levam &6muito tempo&r para serem feitas.

É &dLuV&r, não &4LUV&r.
quests.development.zpm.title: Circuitos &cZPM&r
quests.development.zpm.desc: Os circuitos ao longo desta linha são &cZPM&r e são intercambiáveis em receitas.
quests.development.uv.title: Circuitos &3UV&r
quests.development.uv.desc: Os circuitos ao longo desta linha são &3UV&r e são intercambiáveis em receitas.
quests.development.uhv.title: Circuitos &4UHV&r
quests.development.uhv.desc:
Parabéns! Você chegou ao fim da progressão do circuito.

O único circuito &4UHV&r é o &6Wetware Mainframe&r, que é usado para criar alguns itens do final do jogo.
quests.development.hull_lv.title: Pode ser fabricado na &6Idade do Vapor&r e no &7LV&r
quests.development.hull_lv.subtitle: Feito à mão e artesanalmente
quests.development.hull_lv.desc:
&7Os circuitos do início do jogo são feitos usando artesanato e criação manuais.

Tenha uma boa configuração do &3Create&r para torná-los mais fáceis de produzir e, em seguida, pegue o &3Montador de Circuitos&r para torná-los muito mais eficientes.
quests.development.hull_mv.title: Craftável em &bMV&r
quests.development.hull_mv.subtitle: Dinâmica Integrada
quests.development.hull_mv.desc.1:
A Linha de Circuitos &9Integrados&r pode não ser uma atualização rigorosa em termos de custo, mas servirá como um trampolim para o próximo nível.

Eles exigirão um &3Gravador à Laser&r MV e um &3Máquina de Corte&r MV.
quests.development.hull_mv.desc.2: &l&3Lore:&r&o No Omnifactory, e previamente no Gregicality, essa linha de circuito foi chamada de &9Refinada&r e foi padronizada como níveis posteriores.
quests.development.hull_hv.title: Craftável em &bMV&r e &6HV&r
quests.development.hull_hv.subtitle: Eles tendem a ser pequenos
quests.development.hull_hv.desc:
Os circuitos &eMicro&r são onde a &3Sala Limpa&r se torna um requisito para progredir.

Não seja pego pelo &aMainframe&r - ele requer um &3Montador de Circuitos HV&r.3
quests.development.hull_ev.title: Craftável em &5EV&r
quests.development.hull_ev.subtitle: Era Nano
quests.development.hull_ev.desc:
Esta é a Linha de Circuitos &9Nano&r.

Você pode precisar ir ao &bespaço&r para criá-los.

A partir daqui, a progressão dos circuitos seguirá um padrão:

&9-&r Processador
&9-&r Montagem
&9-&r Supercomputador
&9-&r Mainframe
quests.development.hull_iv.title: Craftável em &1IV&r
quests.development.hull_iv.subtitle: GregTech fica Quântico
quests.development.hull_iv.desc:
Bem-vindo à Linha de Circuitos &9Quânticos&r.

Você pode querer investir em &aPBI&r neste momento, pois os &dSMDs avançados&r irão impulsionar sua produção de circuitos.
quests.development.hull_luv.title: Craftável em &dLuV&r
quests.development.hull_luv.subtitle: Cristalização
quests.development.hull_luv.desc:
Esta é a Linha de Circuitos &9Cristal&r.

Ela está bloqueada atrás da &3Fusão&r.
quests.development.hull_zpm.title: Craftável em &cZPM&r
quests.development.hull_zpm.subtitle: Sofuto ando Wetto, Gappy
quests.development.hull_zpm.desc:
Esta é a Linha de Circuitos &9Wetware&r.

Será o maior desafio de automação a ser enfrentado neste modpack. Boa sorte.
quests.extreme_voltage.subtitle: Reach Mars, discover Nuclear Fission, and build new massive multiblocks
quests.extreme_voltage.welcome_to_ev.des:
You’ve built new &6chemistry lines&r, manufactured advanced &balloys&r, and even launched a &dRocket&r to reach the Moon. We hope you enjoyed the ride, because now things get even more complex, with powerful new machines and demanding processes ahead.

In the &5EV Chapter&r, your main objective will be to craft your very first &1IV&r and &dLuV&r Circuits.

Along the way, several key challenges await you:

• Build your first &6Alloy Blast Smelter&r, a faster version of the EBF, specialized for alloys. With it, you’ll be able to construct your first Large Machine: the &6Large Centrifuge&r, essential for the Nuclear Fission Line. It also lets you upgrade ore processing with a faster Thermal Centrifuge.

• Master our original &eTungsten Line&r - looping all the required materials will test both your knowledge and your patience.

• Prepare to fly to &4Mars&r where you’ll become an &aextraterrestrial farmer&r while also learning to harness the &cpower of the atom&r.

Good luck, this is where GregTech begins to show its true depth!
quests.extreme_voltage.distillation_towe: Mr. President, another Distillation Tower has hit the factory floor

&6The Distillation Tower&r (DT) is a direct multiblock upgrade of the &6Distillery&r. You won't have to choose outputs any more as the DT will handle them all automatically, at an increased power cost.

This machine will be your future bread and butter for anything involving &ePetrochem&r or &eOrganic distillation.

Like the &6Cleanroom&r, the DT can be made taller, though not wider. Starting from the second layer, each added level requires an &9Output Hatch&r and grants one additional fluid output slot; if a level lacks its output hatch, the corresponding fluid will be voided. Count the number of outputs with &dEMI&r so you know how many layers you need. As always, use the Multiblock Preview tab in &dEMI&r and click the multiblock to see where you can place each part.

&cNote:&r Be careful not to over-overclock the DT when producing fuels! Excessive overclocking can reduce the overall power yield, especially for Diesel. Do the math and double-check your calculations.
quests.extreme_voltage.cracker.subtitle: You Crack Me Up
quests.extreme_voltage.cracker.desc:
The &3Cracker&r is a multiblock built with the sole purpose of cracking fluids. It comes with all the recipes of &3Chemical Reactor&r Cracking, but it is &6lossless&r.

This is a great way to save a lot of Fuel in the long term!

Higher Coil tiers provide a small energy discount - up to 70%% with the best Coils available.
quests.extreme_voltage.ev_components.des:
&7By now, you should be getting used to the pattern: with every new Energy Tier comes a fresh batch of components.&r

The &5EV&r tier comes with a few twists - first, &eNeodymium&r. You might already have some stored from your earlier mining trips, but you’ll also find it in large quantities when processing &eMonazite Ore&r.

Secondly, Sensors and Emitters require &bCryogenized Fluix Pearls&r. Hopefully your &dMoon&r infrastructure is up and running to supply them efficiently.

Lastly, Motors require Kanthal wire. If you've been diligently upgrading your EBF, these should be significantly faster to craft than when you made them in MV (and you can recycle your old coils too!), but the Alloy Blast Smelter will be able to make this even faster.
quests.extreme_voltage.assembler.title: EV Assembler
quests.extreme_voltage.assembler.subtitl: Last step before some Extreme power generation!
quests.extreme_voltage.assembler.desc.1:
You know what to do with this by now, but this is a good time to give you a heads up that now is the time to scale up your factory if you haven't already done so.

Take your time to set up AE2 all over your base, making it print you dozens of new machines to keep your factory running at tip-top shape, because if you don't, the rest of EV is going to hit you like a brick wall.

Don't wait to find out the hard way!
quests.extreme_voltage.assembler.desc.2:
At the very least, here's what we recommend before pushing further into EV:

&9-&r Passive production of Rubber, Polyethylene, Oxygen, Hydrogen, Nitrogen, and Chlorine
&9-&r At least two general-purpose EBFs, and a few LCRs
&9-&r AE2 patterns for a circuit of every tier and most of the common crafting components like motors, pumps, plates, and wires
&9-&r A dedicated ore processing setup
&9-&r A couple EV amps worth of power generation (see the below quests)

You can do it, you've made it this far already!
quests.extreme_voltage.t2_rocket.title: Rocket 2: Titanium Boogaloo
quests.extreme_voltage.t2_rocket.desc:
This new rocket shouldn’t be too difficult to assemble, but you’ll notice a clear jump in material costs compared to your previous model.

The upgraded &6Rocket Alloys&r now require both &bDistilled Water&r and &bCryogenized Fluix&r for cooling.

Make sure you've fully set up your &eTitanium&r production line, as you're going to need a lot of it going forward! A continuous production line will save you a lot of frustration down the road.
quests.extreme_voltage.mars.title: I'm on &4Mars&r!
quests.extreme_voltage.mars.subtitle: The Red Planet
quests.extreme_voltage.mars.desc.1:
Before landing on &4Mars&r, there are a few important things to keep in mind.

First, like the Moon, &4Mars&r will have some hostile surface mobs as well. If you haven't already figured out how to make your Space Suit stay with you after dying or invested in new EV-tier weaponry, you'll have a rough time.

The planet is mostly a &edesert world&r, dotted with "&aislands&r" where you’ll find &bwater&r, lush fauna, and much safer places to build your first base. Meanwhile, the open desert is extremely dangerous if you don't watch your step, but contains some other crucial resources!
quests.extreme_voltage.mars.desc.2:
All &bfluid veins&r are spread across Mars, except for &eHeavy Ammoniacial Water&r which can only be found outside of the desert. That’s another good reason to set up your base there.

Energy-wise, &4Mars&r will not be kind to you at first.

Nearly every common &6energy generation method&r is disabled or worthless on the planet, which means you’ll have to rely almost entirely on &cFission&r to power your base.

At the very beginning, we recommend bringing along some &bfilled batteries&r such as &bLapotron Crystals&r to get started, and then look towards setting up Thorium Fuel Rods.

Achieving energy stability on Mars will take time and effort, but once your systems are running smoothly, you’ll feel like &9unlimited power&r is right in the palm of your hand.
quests.extreme_voltage.mars.desc.3:
And one last tip: don’t kill everything you see. Some &aanimals&r can be &franched&r, and you’ll definitely need them later in your progression.

Be sure to check out the &aSpace Survival&r chapter for more Mars-related tips too!

Stay alert, plan ahead, and Mars will reward your courage.
quests.extreme_voltage.nuclear_fuel_fact:
The &6Nuclear Fuel Factory&r has one purpose: producing fuel rods for your &cFission Reactor&r.

It supports &9Parallel Hatches&r which you’ll unlock soon but they aren’t mandatory yet. Functionally, it works much like the Pyrolyse Oven: the &ebetter the coils&r you install, the faster the recipes will run.

Since fuel rod production is both &cslow&r and &cexpensive&r, we strongly recommend using the &ahighest tier coils&r available. Don’t waste your time with Cupronickel, you’ll only be nerfing yourself.
quests.extreme_voltage.heavy_water.title: Heavy Water
quests.extreme_voltage.heavy_water.desc:
To run your Fission Reactor, you’ll need a steady supply of &bHeavy Water&r, and there’s only one place to find it.

It’s located in &adeep fluid veins&r found exclusively beneath the &alush islands&r of Mars. Grab your &6Ore Prospector&r, switch it to &bFluid Mode&r, and start scanning.

Because the amount of &bHeavy Water&r required to keep your reactor running is enormous, you should seriously consider investing in a &6Large Centrifuge&r to produce it yourself.

Install a &516A EV Energy Hatch&r, and you’ll be ready for true mass production ensuring your reactor never runs dry.

Reliable cooling means reliable power - build smart!
quests.extreme_voltage.nuclear_turbine.d:
With Fission, you gain access to a brand new &6Dedicated Turbine&r! (The normal Large Steam Turbine can only handle regular Steam.)

It works just like the previous turbines, but similar to the Gas Turbine, it requires at least an &5EV Rotor Holder&r to operate.

The coils inside this turbine must be &7Cupronickel&r, and they don’t affect performance in any way (but we have to admit, they look pretty good).

In this quest, we suggest two &aRotors&r that fit nicely for the turbine at this stage, but feel free to experiment and use any &brotor&r that suits your setup best.
quests.extreme_voltage.reactor_component: Active and Passive ways to cool your reactor

One of the most interesting features of the Fission Reactor is its ability to have blocks added inside to modify its &emaximum heat capacity&r.

You can place certain &6Component Blocks&r within the corners of the reactor, allowing for up to &e20 blocks&r in total. As an example, you can use &9Material Holders&r and insert cooling items with input buses, which help manage heat buildup.

It’s also important to note that Material Holders are the &conly way&r to enable the &brecipe processing&r part of the Fission Reactor.

Smart internal configuration will make the difference between a stable reactor and one on cooldown, so plan your layout carefully!

As discussed earlier, running three &eThorium Rods&r will push your reactor’s heat to around &c420&r, just above the default limit. However, you can safely reach that threshold by improving your &6reactor insulation&r.&r

Adding &e10 Framed Glacial Wool&r or &e20 Framed AES Insulation&r blocks inside the structure will raise your reactor’s Max Heat to &e420&r, allowing you to operate with three Thorium Rods without triggering cooldown mode.&r

Keep in mind, though, that increasing the number of active rods will also accelerate their &adurability loss&r. This means each rod will produce less steam per tick on average, reducing its &aefficiency&r, but on the other hand, it will &bspeed up fission material production&r significantly.

Whether you prefer &aefficiency&r or &bthroughput&r is entirely up to you - that’s the balancing act at the heart of mastering the &6Fission Reactor&r.
quests.extreme_voltage.heat_exchanger.su: All that heat's gotta go somewhere!
quests.extreme_voltage.heat_exchanger.de:
The &6Heat Exchanger&r is a critical component for the next step of your Fission Line - not only for cooling, but also for producing more &eHigh Pressure Steam&r, the steam that powers your Nuclear Turbine. We strongly advise you to use one for each reactor as they can have an hard time processing different recipes.

It features &bPerfect Overclocking&r and &bSubtick Processing&r. If these terms sound unfamiliar, we strongly recommend reviewing the &dGregTech Energy Chapter&r to fully understand how they affect performance.

Don’t hold back! Install a &516A EV Energy Hatch&r right from the start so you’ll never have to worry about speed or throughput limitations.

We also provide you with a &9Machine Controller&r to attach to your Heat Exchanger’s main controller. We’ve noticed that the machine can sometimes disable itself unexpectedly, so be sure to enable &ePrevent Power Failing&r. This will counter the GregTech mechanic that shuts down machines when they momentarily lose power.
quests.extreme_voltage.refrigerant_pelle: A temporary solution to extreme temperatures

The &dPlutonium Fuel Rod&r runs at extremely high temperatures - so high, in fact, that you’ll need to use &bTerraFirmaGuard PA6&r inside your reactor to prevent it from going into cooldown mode.

These pellets are quite expensive to produce, but you should still be able to mass-produce them early on, or at least enough to keep your first few plutonium rods operational. You need approximatively &9256&r to run a full Plutonium Rod.

Later on, if you don’t like constantly crafting these consumables, you’ll gain access to &6advanced reactor components&r. With those installed, your reactor will be able to safely reach up to &e600 Max Heat&r, greatly improving efficiency and stability.

Invest in better internal parts early, to spend less time babysitting your power core.
quests.extreme_voltage.important_info.su: Not an easy place to start
quests.extreme_voltage.important_info.de:
Before you start building your base on &cMars&r, here are a few important tips to help you settle in successfully.&r

Before crafting up an entire base to carry in your pocket, we always recommend having an exploration mission before you start setting down!

When it comes to your Martian infrastructure, there are two main approaches:&r

• &aPreparation-focused&r: Build everything required for your full &6Fission Line&r before establishing your base. This means an enormous material investment, but allows you to be self-sufficient once operational.

• &aProgressive setup&r: Start smaller with your &6Nuclear Fuel Factory&r, your first &6Fission Reactor&r (running on &eThorium&r), and a &6Nuclear Turbine&r. This route is faster to start, but managing energy will be rough until your fission system stabilizes.

Because every kind of combustion-based energy won't work on Mars, you’ll want to bring a good stock of &bLapotron Crystals&r to power your first recipes and oxygen systems.

We also strongly recommend setting up a &6Distillation Tower&r. &eMartian Air&r can be processed into breathable &bAir&r and several other useful gases. Alternatively, everything for Compressed Nitrox can come from the local Semiheavy Ammoniacal Water.

Survival on Mars isn’t about luck, it’s about preparation!
quests.extreme_voltage.dense_lead.title: Faster Dense Lead Plates
quests.extreme_voltage.dense_lead.subtit: Don't expect it for other dense plates
quests.extreme_voltage.dense_lead.desc: Because of the tremendous amount of Dense Lead Plates you will need for Atomic Casings, we offer a new, albeit less efficient, recipe with the help of the Implosion Compressor. Feel free to use it if you don't want to wait forever for your benders.
quests.extreme_voltage.thorium_rod.title: Fuel Rods
Each &6Fuel Rod&r comes with its own set of properties including &adurability&r (how long it lasts in the reactor), &cheat generation&r (how much heat it produces), and even different &eproducts&r and &bsteam output levels&r. You can view all of this information directly in the &aEMI&r tabs for each rod.&r

Before diving into how the Fission Reactor functions, here are some key points to understand:&r

• The &eHeat Percentage&r shown on a fuel rod translates to slightly less than double its value inside the reactor. For example, a &eThorium Rod&r with a &cHeat&r of 70%% will generate just under 140 heat when enough &bHeavy Water&r is pumped into your reactor. This heat increase is &aadditive&r, meaning that 2 Thorium Rods will raise your reactor’s total heat to around 280.&r

• &aDurability&r decreases at the same base rate for all rods. However, the higher the reactor’s temperature, the faster the rods degrade. Because of this, even if a certain rod type has 4× the durability of another, it doesn’t necessarily mean it will last 4× longer, as &ctemperature directly impacts longevity&r.&r

Balancing the amount of rods within the reactor will be your first challenge with Fission.

• &6Average Heat&r is simply the mean temperature of the reactor while the rod was inside it. Depending on this &laverage heat value&r, the depleted rod will yield &bdifferent output products&r.

You can browse &bEMI&r to preview the possible results and see exactly what each depleted rod produces at specific temperature ranges.
quests.extreme_voltage.fission_reactor.s: Oh no, it's on cooldown again
quests.extreme_voltage.fission_reactor.d:
To start off, if you’re looking for a deeper dive into &6Fission Reactor&r mechanics including formulas, detailed calculations, and system behavior, check out the quest in the &aGregTech Energy&r chapter. It covers everything you’ll need if you want to get into the math behind the heat and efficiency systems.&r

For now, let’s focus on your very first &6Thorium Reactor&r. Its base &cMax Heat&r is &e400&r. As explained in the previous quest, running two Thorium Rods will push your internal heat to around &e280&r, which is perfectly safe. However, adding a third rod raises the total to about &c420 Heat&r, exceeding the limit and triggering &ccooldown mode&r.

Don’t worry, there’s no explosion, but your reactor will shut down and won’t restart until it fully cools back to 0 Heat. This will completely halt both your &aenergy production&r and your &afission product&r generation - a major setback.

Also be aware that each time your reactor enters cooldown mode, every fuel rod inside will receive &c10 000 Heat&r, which can lead to extremely poor outputs or even disastrous losses. Be very cautious, as this is something you absolutely want to avoid.

Managing how many rods are active will therefore be your first &6challenge&r and the main minigame of Fission.

There are multiple ways to approach this, and experimentation is part of the fun.

One way is by attaching a &6Redstone Port&r to your reactor, and using a &aWrench&r on it switches its monitoring mode between &erod count&r and &cheat level&r.

Finally, note that all rods are stored inside the &6Fuel Holder&r. That means limiting the number of active rods isn’t as simple as having 2 rods in the input bus, &cyou’ll need to control what enters the Input Bus&r to fine-tune your reactor’s operation.
quests.extreme_voltage.high_pressure_ste:
&4Important Info&r: If the reactor doesn't have enough space to output High Pressure Steam, it will simply void.

For balancing (and pipe throughput) reasons, the &6Fission Reactor&r does not produce regular steam. Instead, it generates &bHigh Pressure Steam&r, which can only be used in a &6Nuclear Steam Turbine&r.

Attempting to run it through a standard Steam Turbine will &cnot work&r.
quests.extreme_voltage.ev_superconductor:
Now that you've processed some &eRadon&r, you should check out the &5EV Superconductors&r.

These superconductors have higher amp capacity, helping you move energy efficiently across your base and ensuring your &6Nuclear Steam Turbines&r output reaches all your machines without loss.
quests.extreme_voltage.uraninite_dust.de:
To craft your &eUranium Fuel Rods&r, you’ll need a substantial amount of &eUraninite&r - around &c240 Dust&r per rod. Start mining it early to keep up with demand later.

The largest veins can be found on Mars, where some deposits reach &aimpressive sizes&r. Later in your progression, you’ll be able to produce Pitchblende and Uraninite Dust &ainfinitely&r using the &6Ostrum Linear Accelerator&r, but we’ll cover that in more detail in a future quest.
quests.extreme_voltage.uranium_line.subt: That's one more new processing line
quests.extreme_voltage.uranium_line.desc:
To process &eUranium&r and its &eUranium 235&r &eIsotope&r, you’ll need to set up a dedicated small processing line. 

Rest assured, you can &aloop the Fluorine&r, so the only input you’ll need is &eUraninite Dusts&r.

You can build this line on Earth, but it may be more convenient to operate it on Mars, where the majority of Uraninite Ore should be harvested.
quests.extreme_voltage.radioactive_waste: One man's trash is another man's progression material
